issue: Tauri/macOS Finder drag/drop can deliver native file paths instead of browser dataTransfer.files, so the existing HTML drop handler could miss release app file drops.

codeChanges.change: On Tauri DragDrop::Drop, allow each dropped file/directory in tauri-plugin-fs scope so the frontend can read only user-dropped paths.

Register getCurrentWebviewWindow().onDragDropEvent in Tauri, detect whether the native drop landed inside the composer, then stage converted File objects through the existing attachment pipeline. Root cause found on 2026-05-05: the release main surface is a Tauri WebviewWindow, while listening to getCurrentWebview() can miss synthesized window-level drag/drop events. Later focus-safe passes made the hit-test accept both raw Tauri drop coordinates and devicePixelRatio-normalized coordinates, then expanded the native drop target from the composer rectangle to the full chat panel so Finder drops are not lost when the user lands on message history instead of the input.
Convert native dropped paths to File objects via @tauri-apps/plugin-fs readFile/stat with extension-based MIME inference and visible per-file errors.
Keep browser builds aliased away from the Tauri webview API.
Keep browser builds aliased away from the Tauri webviewWindow API.
Alias @tauri-apps/api/webviewWindow to a browser-only polyfill outside the Tauri frontend build.

verification.webBuild: pass: pnpm --filter @offisim/web build via desktop build:frontend
postPatchChecks: pass: pnpm exec biome check packages/ui-office/src/components/chat/ChatInput.tsx packages/ui-office/src/components/chat/MessageBubble.tsx packages/ui-office/src/components/chat/ChatPanel.tsx apps/web/src/polyfills/tauri-api-webview-window.ts apps/web/vite.config.ts
verification.releaseProcess: successful foreground pass used release pid 81226; Computer Use attached the exact release app after a system-level Finder drag/drop from a visible Finder window to the release chat panel

successfulDragDrop.stagedChip: Computer Use observed release app staged chip: sample.md, 160 B, Text, 160 chars

attempts: Computer Use Finder-to-Offisim drag using raw screenshot coordinates
Computer Use Finder-to-Offisim drag using Finder-window-offset-adjusted target coordinates
CoreGraphics mouse drag from Finder sample.docx row to release composer after raising Finder with Computer Use

Post-root-cause-fix release app was rebuilt and launched from the exact .app path. One attempt showed the SDK-lane attachment guard, proving native drop events can reach attachment handling, but this does not satisfy 12.8 because the session mode was not the Gateway path and no normal staged chip was proven.

After the coordinate hit-test compatibility fix, the release app was rebuilt again and launched from the exact .app path. The live Finder drag/drop attempt was stopped because repeated foreground Finder/Offisim control disrupted the user's active work session.

Latest attempt on release pid 57314: Finder selected packages/doc-engine/harness/fixtures/sample.md, then Computer Use drag was attempted from the selected Finder row toward the Offisim composer at multiple start coordinates after revealing the Name column. Offisim remained READY and no staged chip appeared, so the tool-level drag attempt did not satisfy native Finder drag/drop verification.

After expanding the native drop hit target to the full chat panel, rebuilt release pid 80198 from the exact worktree app path. Finder selected packages/doc-engine/harness/fixtures/sample.md and Computer Use drag was attempted from the selected Finder row toward both the Offisim composer and the broader chat panel. Offisim remained READY and no staged chip appeared, so this still does not satisfy native Finder drag/drop verification.

Final pass on release pid 81226: used a visible Finder window source row for packages/doc-engine/harness/fixtures/sample.md and a system-level mouse drag into the release app chat panel. Computer Use observed the release staged chip sample.md with 160 B / Text / 160 chars. Sending it persisted attachment 3f3104bc-6505-4371-98bf-a55ec29d4013; .bin wc -c was 160, .meta.json byteLength was 160, and .bin sha256 matched both .meta.json and the source fixture. A follow-up release task called read_attachment audit ma-d99c8cab-4787-44ab-b0e6-168c2093f7dd and returned # Doc-Engine Fixture plus 160 bytes.
releaseDragDropAttempts.observedComputerUseState: Before the root-cause fix, the exact release app remained attached and usable, but the composer still showed no newly staged dragged file after the attempts. After the getCurrentWebviewWindow listener fix, one drop surfaced the typed attachment lane guard. After the coordinate hit-test and chat-panel target fixes, the final system-level Finder drag/drop staged sample.md in the release composer and the release app returned to READY after read_attachment verification.
releaseDragDropAttempts.conclusion: OpenSpec 12.8 is complete. The release app accepted a native Finder drag/drop, persisted raw bytes through the Tauri attachment store, wrote matching metadata, and read the dragged attachment through the Gateway lane.
